hypothesis: Introducing aromatic residues at position 5 and hydrophobic substitutions at position 11 will enhance SSTR2-specific interactions with ECL2 and TM5 while avoiding off-target (SSTR3/SSTR5) binding through steric differentiation.

critic_feedback_used.hypothesis: The hypothesis is revised to emphasize that high ddG alone is insufficient for success; selectivity (Δmargin > 0) is the primary success criterion. Design must now prioritize disrupting off-target contacts at non-pharmacophore positions.

proposed_changes.rationale: To improve selectivity, we must disrupt conserved-pocket contacts at non-pharmacophore positions (1/2/5/6/11/12) that are likely responsible for off-target binding to SSTR3/SSTR5.
Prioritizing selectivity over ddG alone will ensure that candidates are not only strong binders but also selective for SSTR2 over other SSTR subtypes.
